sections.summary: Rifampin strongly induces the metabolism of ethinylestradiol, which can lower contraceptive hormone levels enough to cause breakthrough bleeding and contraceptive failure.
sections.mechanism: Rifampin is a potent inducer of CYP3A4 and other hepatic enzymes, accelerating the metabolism of ethinylestradiol and progestins, which lowers circulating hormone concentrations below levels needed to reliably suppress ovulation.
sections.clinical_effects: Reduced contraceptive efficacy manifests as breakthrough bleeding and, more importantly, an increased risk of unintended pregnancy during and shortly after rifampin therapy.
sections.management: Advise patients on rifampin to use an additional or alternative non-hormonal contraceptive method (e.g., barrier method) during treatment and for a period afterward, since enzyme induction can persist after the antibiotic is stopped.
sections.monitoring: No specific lab test detects this interaction; counsel patients directly and monitor for breakthrough bleeding as a signal of reduced hormonal effect.

evidence.citation: Rifadin (rifampin) FDA prescribing information, Drug Interactions section
